name: Makapu'u Lighthouse Trail
tagline: Fully paved and stroller-friendly with whale sightings Nov–May and dramatic ocean cliffs.
parking: Free lot off Kalanianaole Hwy (fills by 9am weekends)
hours: Sunrise to sunset daily (parking lot gate opens at sunrise)
bestTime: Jan–Mar for peak whale watching; early morning to avoid heat and crowds
talkStory: The lighthouse at the summit has been guiding ships since 1909 and houses one of the largest lighthouse lenses in the country — a hyper-radiant Fresnel lens over 8 feet tall, shipped from Paris. The trail was originally built as an access road for lighthouse keepers who lived at the point. During whale season (Jan-Mar), this is the single best land-based whale watching spot in Hawaii — humpbacks breach so close you can hear the splash. The tidepools below the lookout harbor rare species, but only scramble down when surf is calm. Fully paved and stroller-accessible.
